skill.name: file-converter
skill.description: Convert documents, structured data, and image files between common formats using Python libraries and selected command-line converters.
skill.summary: Convert documents, data files, and images with format-specific Python guidance.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many dangerous patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown fences, inline code formatting, and normal file conversion examples. The remaining risk is medium because the skill intentionally generates and executes conversion code that reads and writes files, and it may use external conversion tools such as LibreOffice CLI or potrace. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, malware intent, secrets, or network behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: Generated Conversion Code Writes User Files
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to generate and execute conversion code, and examples write output files. This is expected for a converter, but it can overwrite files or process unsafe input if paths are not checked.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write examples and workflow are explicit. The behavior is legitimate for file conversion, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: External Conversion Tools May Be Invoked
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends LibreOffice CLI and potrace for some conversions. These tools are legitimate, but command construction must avoid untrusted shell interpolation and should run only on intended files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced command-line tools are clearly present. The examples use fixed filenames, so command injection is not demonstrated, but generated commands still require care.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external command detections come from Markdown code fences and inline package names, not Ruby shell backtick execution. No Ruby code or malicious shell wrapper was found in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows Markdown examples and inline formatting. I did not find evidence of Ruby backtick execution.

low_findings.title: High-Severity Static Patterns Are Benign Text
low_findings.description: The certificate, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance detections do not match the surrounding content. They appear to be words inside tables or prose about conversion libraries, not secrets, crypto code, or scanning behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary documentation text at these lines. No evidence found of keys, weak cryptographic implementation, or reconnaissance logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Conversion Code Writes User Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to generate and execute conversion code, and examples write output files. This is expected for a converter, but it can overwrite files or process unsafe input if paths are not checked.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write examples and workflow are explicit. The behavior is legitimate for file conversion, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Conversion Tools May Be Invoked
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends LibreOffice CLI and potrace for some conversions. These tools are legitimate, but command construction must avoid untrusted shell interpolation and should run only on intended files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced command-line tools are clearly present. The examples use fixed filenames, so command injection is not demonstrated, but generated commands still require care.

content.value_statement: Teams often need reliable conversions across documents, data files, and images without rebuilding each workflow. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code format-specific guidance for generating and running conversion steps.
actual_capabilities: Converts common document formats such as Markdown, HTML, PDF, DOCX, and text.
Converts structured data between JSON, CSV, YAML, XML, and TOML formats.
Converts raster and vector image formats including PNG, JPG, WebP, SVG, and GIF.
Selects Python libraries and command-line tools based on source and target formats.
Handles edge cases such as encodings, transparency, color profiles, and nested data.
limitations: Some conversions require optional libraries or system tools to be installed first.
PDF and DOCX conversions may lose layout details or styling fidelity.
Raster to SVG tracing has limited accuracy for photographs and complex artwork.
Generated code must be reviewed before processing sensitive or untrusted files.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reports for Publishing
use_cases.description: Convert Markdown or HTML reports into PDFs or clean HTML exports for distribution.

use_cases.title: Move Data Between Tools
use_cases.description: Transform JSON, CSV, YAML, XML, or TOML files while preserving useful structure.

use_cases.title: Create Web-Ready Images
use_cases.description: Convert and optimize images for web use while handling transparency and color modes.

prompt_templates.title: Convert One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single conversion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert input.md to output.html. Preserve headings, lists, tables, and fenced code blocks.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Data With Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert customers.csv to customers.json. Infer numbers and booleans where safe, and keep empty cells as null values.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Images for Web
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for image output control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert all PNG files in input_images to WebP in output_images. Use balanced quality and preserve transparency.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Complex Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with review before execution.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the best conversion path from DOCX to PDF on Linux, list required tools, then generate the commands for my files.

output_examples.input: Request: Convert a Markdown project brief to HTML.
output_examples.output: The skill selects a Markdown parser, enables table and fenced block support, writes an HTML file, and reports the output path.

output_examples.input: Request: Convert a CSV export to JSON.
output_examples.output: The skill reads the header row, converts rows into records, applies safe type inference, and writes formatted JSON.

output_examples.input: Request: Convert a PNG logo to WebP.
output_examples.output: The skill uses Pillow, preserves alpha transparency, sets a practical quality value, and saves a WebP copy.

best_practices: Use explicit input and output paths, and confirm before overwriting existing files.
Install only the libraries needed for the requested conversion.
Review generated code before running it on private, large, or untrusted files.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated shell commands with unsanitized filenames or wildcards.
Do not expect PDF, DOCX, or raster to vector conversions to preserve every visual detail.
Do not process sensitive files without checking output paths and temporary files.

faq.question: Which file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It covers common document, data, and image formats, including PDF, DOCX, Markdown, HTML, JSON, CSV, YAML, XML, PNG, JPG, WebP, SVG, and GIF.

faq.question: Does it install conversion tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend libraries or tools, but the user or agent environment must install missing dependencies.

faq.question: Can it preserve complex document layouts?
faq.answer: Only partly. HTML to PDF usually preserves styling better than PDF to text or DOCX to Markdown.

faq.question: Can it batch convert images?
faq.answer: Yes. The references include batch patterns for processing image folders with Pillow.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted files?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review generated code and avoid running external converters on files from unknown sources without isolation.

faq.question: Can it convert nested JSON to CSV?
faq.answer: Yes, but nested structures must be flattened or serialized because CSV is a table format.
